subject: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 17 Oct

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Pls find SIP weekly update attached for your review.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 6

   - For CNSIP, is growth coming from Taobao sellers or existing SLS
   sellers who are not in all markets?
- Any other levers to grow faster?

Slide 7

   - Where did we land on the ownership of Direct selling?

Where
   sellers/brands complain that they often get passed around
   - How are we tracking the use of paid ads right now for direct selling?
- How are returns being handled right now for SIP and Direct Selling?

Is
   it a blocker for sellers to join?

Others

   - What other lanes have not been launched yet?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 6

   -

   For CNSIP, is growth coming from Taobao sellers or existing SLS sellers
   who are not in all markets?
   -

      CNSIP reporting includes only existing SLS sellers who are not in all
      markets, excludes Taobao sellers from Swarm project, we will
start to track
      Swarm performance from next week
      -

      Within this scope, the growth is mainly driven by incubation of new
      SKUs migrated via our revamp efforts



   -

   Any other levers to grow faster?
   -

      We have maximized SKU count via migration, and now focus on driving
      up SKU efficiency via
      -

         Price reduction for non-performing SKUs; target ADO uplift ~3k;
         ETA: end-Nov
         -

         AMS coverage ramp up in PH/VN (80% coverage in SG/MY/TH done);
         target ADO uplift ~1k; ETA: end-Oct
         -

         Optimize marketing budget ROI: target ADO uplift ~1k by Dec



Slide 7

   -

   Where did we land on the ownership of Direct selling? Where
   sellers/brands complain that they often get passed around
   -

      As aligned with local market CB teams, ownership is structured as
      below
      -

         Export market CB team: owns seller acquisition and support
         -

         Import market CB team: owns seller programs (treat direct sellers
         the same way as CN/KR/JPCB MP sellers)
         -

         Reg SIP team: reg direct selling PMO
         -

      Seller/brand enquiries SOPs are now established in MY/SG/ID/VN/TH as
      below
      -

         Managed sellers: seller’s local RM >> export market CB team >> reg
         SIP team [channel: seatalk group chat with local RMs]
         -

         Unmanaged sellers: seller’s local CS >> export market CB team >>
         reg SIP team [channel: CS IHS system]



   -

   How are we tracking the use of paid ads right now for direct selling?
   -

      Reg direct selli

content.question: - Any other levers to grow faster?

- We have maximized SKU count via migration, and now focus on driving up
      SKU efficiency via


   - Price reduction for non-performing SKUs; target ADO uplift ~3k; ETA:
         end-Nov
         - AMS coverage ramp up in PH/VN (80% coverage in SG/MY/TH done);
         target ADO uplift ~1k; ETA: end-Oct
         - Optimize marketing budget ROI: target ADO uplift ~1k by Dec





Are there still sellers who are not on SIP if they are not doing direct
selling in the market?

Slide 7

   - Where did we land on the ownership of Direct selling?

Where
   sellers/brands complain that they often get passed around


   - As aligned with local market CB teams, ownership is structured as below


   - Export market CB team: owns seller acquisition and support
         - Import market CB team: owns seller programs (treat direct
         sellers the same way as CN/KR/JPCB MP sellers)
         - Reg SIP team: reg direct selling PMO


   - Seller/brand enquiries SOPs are now established in MY/SG/ID/VN/TH as
      below


   - Managed sellers: seller’s local RM >> export market CB team >> reg SIP
         team [channel: seatalk group chat with local RMs]
         - Unmanaged sellers: seller’s local CS >> export market CB team >>
         reg SIP team [channel: CS IHS system]





This is as good as saying there is no owner.



   - How are we tracking the use of paid ads right now for direct selling?

- Reg direct selling PMO owns the tracking of paid ads spending and
      shares the insights with export market PICs to drive seller investment
      - At current stage, we focus on pushing sellers to start investing on
      ads, thus key metrics we track are 1) # sellers on ads; 2) ads
spending; 3)
      ads GMV; 4) ROAS. An example of IDMY tracking as below



Do we have the breakdown by lane…adoption, tr% etc.



   - How are returns being handled right now for SIP and Direct Selling?

Is
   it a blocker for sellers to join?

- Pls see the summary below for short-term/as-is. Variation in practices
      for direct selling mainly due to 1) export market request; 2) feasibility
      of establishing a RTS channel.

o   Short-term, RR scheme is not a key blocker for ID/VN/MY sellers to join
SIP/Direct, based on seller feedback. We will monitor seller sentiment with
local market PICs and enhance seller education to help them price in the
potential RR cost.



Why do we choose for local disposal instead of RTS?
